paper_title: Phosphate solubilization, indole-3-acetic acid synthesis and nitrogen fixation ability of various indigenous microorganism communities from different agriecosystem habitats

entities.id: Phosphate solubilization, indole-3-acetic acid synthesis and nitrogen fixation ability of various indigenous microorganism communities from different agriecosystem habitats
entities.description: Title and main research problem of the study focusing on microbial functionalities.
The process of dissolving insoluble phosphate compounds into soluble forms.
Biosynthesis of a plant growth hormone by microorganisms.
The conversion of atmospheric nitrogen into ammonia or related nitrogenous compounds.
The ability of soil to sustain plant growth by providing essential nutrients.
Consortia of beneficial fungi and bacteria collected from various soils.
National Botanical Research Institute's Phosphate liquid medium used for phosphate solubilization testing.
Insoluble phosphorus source used in the experiments.
Amino acid supplement used as a precursor for IAA synthesis.
Nitrogen-free medium used to test biological nitrogen fixation.
Polymerase chain reaction used to amplify nifH gene sequences.
Specific primers used for detecting the nifH functional gene.
Statistical software used for data analysis.
Numerical data collected from 14 individual habitats and 1 mixed community.
Highest level of dissolved phosphate achieved in the study.
Maximum concentration of IAA produced by IMOCs with tryptophan.
Range of total nitrogen fixed by eight IMOCs.
The length of the amplified nifH gene fragment.
Primary research field concerning crop production and soil health.
Researcher from Biotechnology Research and Development Institute.
Researcher from Biotechnology Research and Development Institute.
Researcher from the Department of Soil Science.
